tasks.task: Confirm the execution baseline and record the exact master-plan revision used by the team.
tasks.definition_of_done: A signed baseline record links product scope, locked decisions, and every later implementation artifact.

tasks.task: Define “finished product” as Foundation plus Professional Expansion; record excluded candidates such as cloud collaboration, multicam, public marketplace, nodal compositing, mobile editing, and hosted rendering.
tasks.definition_of_done: Scope matrix has explicit in/out/deferred rows and an approval owner.

tasks.task: Create the product requirements document with user stories, failure stories, and measurable acceptance criteria.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every in-scope feature has at least one normal-flow and one failure-flow acceptance case.

tasks.task: Create the architecture decision record index and copy all locked decisions into non-editable baseline ADRs.
tasks.definition_of_done: ADR index covers conversation surface, local-first model, timeline authority, rational timing, audio authority, revisions, trust policy, and wrapper policy.

tasks.task: Create a requirements-to-test traceability matrix.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every requirement maps to a phase, owner, test class, and release gate.

tasks.task: Define workstreams, code owners, review owners, and escalation rules for product/UI, timeline, server/media, preview/adapters, bridge, render/QA, security, and release.
tasks.definition_of_done: Repository ownership and required-review rules are documented.

tasks.task: Define issue states and evidence requirements: Planned, Ready, In Progress, Blocked, Review, Accepted, Rejected.
tasks.definition_of_done: No task can be marked Accepted without linked code, tests, and acceptance evidence.

tasks.task: Open and maintain a risk register for timing drift, preview mismatch, cross-engine bridges, cache correctness, audio drift, upstream churn, licensing, and isolation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Each risk has an owner, detection signal, mitigation, and blocking threshold.

tasks.task: Define change-control rules for revising locked decisions or promoting deferred features.
tasks.definition_of_done: Any change requires an ADR, impact analysis, migration/test plan, and explicit approval.

tasks.task: Create a release-signoff checklist that separates encoded, QA-passed, approved, and delivered states.
tasks.definition_of_done: Release workflow cannot treat successful encoding as delivery.

tasks.task: Map the primary end-to-end user journeys: create/open, import, edit, inspect/capture, request Codex changes, compare, render, QA, approve, and recover.
tasks.definition_of_done: Each journey has happy path, loading, empty, warning, error, and recovery states.

tasks.task: Define information architecture for Edit, Inspect, Media, Animation, and Deliver workspaces.
tasks.definition_of_done: Workspace map identifies persistent global state and workspace-specific panels.

tasks.task: Freeze the desktop shell: top status bar, collapsible media/project browser, program/source monitor, inspector, multitrack timeline, and status footer.
tasks.definition_of_done: Layout supports resizable panels and preserves monitor/timeline priority.

tasks.task: Create design tokens for surfaces, borders, spacing, typography, icon sizing, focus rings, selected states, warnings, errors, success, and engine indicators.
tasks.definition_of_done: Tokens are machine-readable and cover light-independent initial dark theme.

tasks.task: Specify component states for buttons, fields, sliders, tabs, tree rows, list/grid assets, clips, track headers, badges, progress, toasts, dialogs, and contextual menus.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every component documents default, hover, focus, active, disabled, busy, warning, and error states where applicable.

tasks.task: Define timeline interaction rules for selection, multi-selection, drag thresholds, trim handles, snapping, ripple mode, modifier keys, auto-scroll, and collision feedback.
tasks.definition_of_done: Rules are unambiguous enough to implement without inventing behavior in code review.

tasks.task: Define monitor behavior for fit/fill, zoom, pan, safe zones, guides, checkerboard, fullscreen, selected-layer highlight, A/B comparison, and capture menus.
tasks.definition_of_done: Monitor spec distinguishes interactive preview, rendered fidelity, isolated capture, and comparison states.

tasks.task: Define inspector progressive disclosure for common, Remotion-native, HyperFrames-native, bridge, multi-selection, asset, audio, and caption contexts.
tasks.definition_of_done: Unsafe mixed edits and native-animation limitations are explicitly represented.

tasks.task: Define keyboard command routing, focus ownership, text-entry exceptions, configurable shortcut model, and conflict handling.
tasks.definition_of_done: Shortcut table covers transport, edit tools, marks, capture, markers, undo/redo, zoom, and navigation.

tasks.task: Define accessibility requirements for keyboard operation, screen-reader labels, high contrast, reduced motion, scalable text, non-color-only status, and focus restoration.
tasks.definition_of_done: Accessibility acceptance checks exist for every major workspace.

tasks.task: Create high-fidelity samples for Edit, Inspect/Codex Bridge, Deliver/QA, Media/Source, and Animation/Bridge workspaces.
tasks.definition_of_done: Five reviewed samples exist in HTML and PNG form with implementation notes.

tasks.task: Create a UI specification that maps every visible control to a command, query, or event contract.
tasks.definition_of_done: No production control is decorative or backed by an undefined operation.

tasks.task: Select and pin the spike versions of Node.js, package manager, browser/runtime, FFmpeg build strategy, Remotion family, and HyperFrames family.
tasks.definition_of_done: Lockfile and environment manifest reproduce the spike on the supported machine.

tasks.task: Create the canonical mixed-engine fixture with one Remotion composition, one HyperFrames composition, raw video, image, captions, voiceover, music, alpha content, and a 30000/1001 timeline.
tasks.definition_of_done: Fixture includes known frame anchors, audio cues, bridge boundaries, and expected hashes/metrics.

tasks.task: Prototype the rational-rate integer-frame master clock with half-open ranges and explicit rounding boundaries.
tasks.definition_of_done: Repeated frame mapping produces identical source-frame and audio-sample results.

tasks.task: Prototype synchronized play, pause, seek, frame-step, loop, drift reporting, and hard resynchronization across both engines.
tasks.definition_of_done: Measured drift meets the recorded spike budget or produces an explicit architecture revision.

tasks.task: Prototype exact native still capture from Remotion and HyperFrames.
tasks.definition_of_done: Both engines render the selected authoritative master frame repeatedly with deterministic identity under the strict environment.

tasks.task: Prototype mixed-layer interactive preview with shared media and visible approximation warnings.
tasks.definition_of_done: The program monitor can present layered engine and shared-media output without independent uncontrolled clocks.

tasks.task: Test transparent intermediate formats and image-sequence fallback on target hardware.
tasks.definition_of_done: Alpha mode, bit depth, decode path, quality, speed, and disk cost are measured and one default plus fallback is selected.

tasks.task: Spike Remotion as the initial replaceable finishing compositor for mixed-engine delivery.
tasks.definition_of_done: A mixed-engine range reaches final encode through the proposed render-core boundary.

tasks.task: Spike shared Web Audio preview and deterministic offline/FFmpeg audio mix from one authoritative graph.
tasks.definition_of_done: Long-fixture sample boundaries and drift are measured; preview/final parameter mapping is documented.

tasks.task: Prototype atomic coordinated revision commits, optimistic concurrency, stale-lock handling, crash before pointer swap, and orphan recovery.
tasks.definition_of_done: Crash tests always leave the prior committed revision authoritative.

tasks.task: Prototype source-edit begin/commit/abort and quarantine of unwrapped external source changes.
tasks.definition_of_done: Native source patches become one reversible project transaction or remain non-authoritative.

tasks.task: Prototype trusted and imported-untrusted composition isolation with filesystem, network, environment, CPU, memory, process, wall-time, and output limits.
tasks.definition_of_done: Imported executable content cannot access denied resources and policy violations are observable.

tasks.task: Define strict and compatible preview environment fingerprints and collect the first real fingerprints.
tasks.definition_of_done: Fingerprint input list and reuse rules are frozen for final vs non-final artifacts.

tasks.task: Benchmark cold start, project open, warm seek, frame-step, native still, memory, GPU use, preview drift, audio drift, and render throughput.
tasks.definition_of_done: Evidence-backed budgets replace provisional assumptions.

tasks.task: Consolidate spike results into candidate ADRs for adapter v1, schema v1, timing, compositor, isolation, environment identity, and measured performance/fidelity budgets.
tasks.definition_of_done: Candidate decisions include evidence, rejected alternatives, limitations, and unresolved blockers ready for cross-cutting contract freeze.

tasks.task: Freeze the authoritative audio-transport contract: scheduler-owned time, audio start/seek barriers, scrub and J/K/L behavior, playback-rate policy, drift measurement/correction, latency reporting, and native-engine audio suppression.
tasks.definition_of_done: Preview and audio packages can integrate without circular ownership; one documented policy governs interactive transport and final sample mapping.

tasks.task: Freeze the command-authorization contract for UI, API, CLI, and Codex: read-only capabilities, normal mutations, destructive operations, idempotency/replay, actor/session identity, rate categories, and audit fields.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every command class has one authorization level and no later phase needs an informal “security policy” dependency.

tasks.task: Freeze the QA/approval/delivery lifecycle contract: `rendered_unchecked`, `qa_failed`, `qa_warning`, `qa_passed`, `approved`, and `delivered`, including invalidation, exceptions, actors, timestamps, and required evidence.
tasks.definition_of_done: UI, API, render, QA, approvals, and receipts share one transition table; encoding alone can never produce `delivered`.

tasks.task: Freeze the caption-render interface: deterministic caption layer plan, font/glyph dependencies, timing/highlight evaluation, burn-in compositor input, subtitle-file output, and QA anchors.
tasks.definition_of_done: Caption production is independent of the final encoder and exposes stable artifacts for preview, compositor, delivery, and QA.

tasks.task: Freeze the executable-content trust and worker-isolation interface: trust classes, approved roots, network/environment policy, read-only mounts, process/resource limits, cache separation, policy identity, and violation reports.
tasks.definition_of_done: Adapters and render workers can implement against a stable policy while P23 later hardens and adversarially verifies enforcement.

tasks.task: Freeze the privacy, redaction, diagnostics-retention, and support-bundle contract, including project-relative paths, secret patterns, preview-before-export, and zero unsolicited telemetry.
tasks.definition_of_done: Bridge, logs, receipts, and support bundles use one redaction policy without a diagnostics/privacy dependency cycle.

tasks.task: Freeze the shared preflight rule-engine contract for schema, assets, fonts, capabilities, proxies, alpha, audio, rights, trust policy, disk, output paths, and environment compatibility.
tasks.definition_of_done: Render planning, Deliver UI, and QA can consume the same rule/result model while rule implementations mature incrementally.

tasks.task: Freeze the source-monitor scope boundary: Foundation source inspection versus Professional source editing, including the master-clock isolation and command ownership rules.
tasks.definition_of_done: Foundation contains viewing, independent scrub/frame-step, metadata, and safe prop/variable audition; source marks and edit insertion are reserved for P25.

tasks.task: Freeze the render-receipt and evidence contract covering revision, job, profile, engines/adapters, strict environment, dependency hashes, cache lineage, outputs, audio measurements, QA, exceptions, approval, and reproduction inputs.
tasks.definition_of_done: Render, Deliver, QA, and release phases share one receipt contract and do not depend on a future informal “receipt policy.”

tasks.task: Publish the cross-cutting contract index, exact task-dependency graph, and Milestone 0 cycle audit; approve or block production implementation.
tasks.definition_of_done: All downstream contracts have versioned owners and schemas; dependency validation reports zero missing IDs, ambiguous placeholders, and cycles.

tasks.task: Create the workspace with apps/studio-web, apps/studio-server, and packages for schema, timeline, preview, engine adapters, media, audio, render, bridge, QA, and UI components.
tasks.definition_of_done: Repository structure matches ownership boundaries and avoids circular dependencies.

tasks.task: Configure strict TypeScript, shared compiler presets, project references, path aliases, and package exports.
tasks.definition_of_done: All packages compile with strict checks and no implicit cross-package private imports.

tasks.task: Configure formatting, linting, import rules, unused-code checks, and commit-time validation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Local and CI enforcement produce identical results.

tasks.task: Select and configure unit, property, integration, visual, and end-to-end test runners.
tasks.definition_of_done: One example test of each class runs in CI.

tasks.task: Create deterministic fixture and golden-artifact directories with checksum manifests.
tasks.definition_of_done: Fixture changes require explicit golden update review.

tasks.task: Create shared error/result types, correlation IDs, structured logging, redaction helpers, and diagnostic categories.
tasks.definition_of_done: Errors cross package/process boundaries without losing cause, stage, entity, or repair hint.

tasks.task: Create workspace scripts for dev, build, typecheck, lint, test, fixture render, QA, clean-cache, and release validation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Commands work from repository root and fail with actionable messages.

tasks.task: Configure CI with dependency caching, lockfile validation, unit/integration jobs, security checks, artifact retention, and protected release gates.
tasks.definition_of_done: Merges cannot bypass required checks or mutate golden artifacts silently.

tasks.task: Add generated-code and schema-build rules so source schemas and generated validators cannot drift.
tasks.definition_of_done: CI detects stale generated outputs.

tasks.task: Create contributor, architecture, debugging, fixture, and test-evidence templates.
tasks.definition_of_done: New implementation work starts from consistent documentation and evidence conventions.

tasks.task: Implement normalized rational serialization and validation, including positive denominators, reduction to lowest terms, and safe bigint encoding.
tasks.definition_of_done: Round-trip tests cover NTSC rates, speed ratios, and invalid values.

tasks.task: Implement schemas for chai.project, timeline, assets, settings, transaction, current-revision pointer, autosave metadata, named versions, and approval state.
tasks.definition_of_done: Runtime and JSON-schema validation agree on valid and invalid fixtures.

tasks.task: Implement semantic whole-project validation beyond structural schema checks.
tasks.definition_of_done: Invalid references, timing, overlaps, source ranges, capabilities, audio graph, and approval transitions fail with entity-specific diagnostics.

tasks.task: Implement the self-contained project-folder initializer and canonical directory structure.
tasks.definition_of_done: A new project contains no authoritative database-only state and is valid immediately.

tasks.task: Implement immutable revision directories and atomically replaced current-revision pointer.
tasks.definition_of_done: Crash injection before/after each write never exposes a partially coordinated revision.

tasks.task: Implement project mutation lock acquisition, ownership, heartbeat, timeout, and stale-lock recovery.
tasks.definition_of_done: Concurrent writers cannot silently overwrite each other.

tasks.task: Implement baseRevisionId optimistic concurrency and structured conflict reports.
tasks.definition_of_done: Stale commands fail without silent rebasing and identify changed entities.

tasks.task: Implement the generic validated command envelope with command ID, actor, timestamp, affected entities, payload schema, and expected revision.
tasks.definition_of_done: All normal authoritative mutations pass through one audited interface.

tasks.task: Implement transaction records with parent revision, before/after hashes, command summary, diff summary, source-edit metadata, and warnings.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every committed revision can explain how and why it was created.

tasks.task: Implement source-edit begin, commit, abort, validation, hash snapshot, diff capture, cache invalidation hooks, and external-change quarantine.
tasks.definition_of_done: Native source changes are reversible and cannot become authoritative outside the protocol.

tasks.task: Implement command inversion and persistent multi-step undo/redo with asynchronous-operation barriers.
tasks.definition_of_done: Undo restores visible and persisted state; render history remains separate.

tasks.task: Implement debounced autosave, pre-risk snapshots, rotating retention, crash detection, and recovery selection.
tasks.definition_of_done: A forced crash recovers the newest valid autosave without overwriting the last approved version.

tasks.task: Implement named immutable versions: Draft, Review, Approved, Delivery Candidate, and Delivered.
tasks.definition_of_done: State transitions obey QA/approval rules and point to immutable revisions.

tasks.task: Implement versioned migration registry, backups, dry-run reports, rollback where practical, and unsupported-version errors.
tasks.definition_of_done: Old fixtures migrate deterministically or fail clearly without silent timing reinterpretation.

tasks.task: Build revision, concurrency, crash, undo, autosave, external-change, and migration test suites.
tasks.definition_of_done: The milestone foundation gate passes repeated close/reopen comparisons with no state drift.

tasks.task: Define stable entity IDs and immutable snapshot types for timelines, tracks, clips, nested sequences, keyframes, markers, transitions, bridges, captions, and automation.
tasks.definition_of_done: No authoritative relation depends on array position or display name.

tasks.task: Implement integer master-frame and half-open range primitives with overflow, bounds, and duration validation.
tasks.definition_of_done: All APIs reject floating-point authority and invalid ranges.

tasks.task: Implement exact timeline-to-source and source-to-timeline rational transforms, floor/ceiling policies, nested rates, speed ratios, and drop-frame display separation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Golden math tests cover 23.976, 29.97, 59.94, nested sequences, and non-unit speed.

tasks.task: Implement track ordering, visual z-order, audio bus order, lock/hide/mute/solo state, and track-type constraints.
tasks.definition_of_done: Derived order is deterministic and validated.

tasks.task: Implement clip placement, overlap rules, source in/out validation, handles, link groups, selection groups, and nested-sequence boundaries.
tasks.definition_of_done: Invalid placements return repairable conflict details.

tasks.task: Implement snapping candidates for playhead, markers, clip boundaries, captions, phrases, keyframes, and user-defined guides.
tasks.definition_of_done: Snapping is frame-exact, priority-ordered, and toggleable.

tasks.task: Implement select, multi-select, move, nudge, insert, overwrite, replace, duplicate, copy/paste, group/ungroup, and link/unlink commands.
tasks.definition_of_done: Each command has validation, inverse behavior, and stable selection outcome.

tasks.task: Implement split/blade, trim in/out, ripple trim, ripple delete, lift, delete, and set/clear in-out range commands.
tasks.definition_of_done: Boundary, locked-track, linked-media, and nested-sequence cases pass tests.

tasks.task: Implement track add/remove/rename/reorder plus clip rename and metadata-edit commands.
tasks.definition_of_done: No command bypasses revision/audit logic.

tasks.task: Implement markers, issue categories, annotations references, approval markers, and navigation indexes.
tasks.definition_of_done: Markers remain stable through ripple and retiming according to documented policy.

tasks.task: Implement common keyframe storage, interpolation metadata, property ownership, and native-animation preservation flags.
tasks.definition_of_done: Generic controls cannot silently overwrite engine-native animation authority.

tasks.task: Implement derived indexes for visible clips, active layers, nearby context, asset usage, transcript phrases, render dependencies, and search.
tasks.definition_of_done: Indexes are rebuildable and never authoritative.

tasks.task: Implement edit history labels and deterministic human-readable diff summaries.
tasks.definition_of_done: Undo menu and transaction log explain every edit in user terms.

tasks.task: Create property-based and fuzz tests for ranges, ripple behavior, nested mappings, selection retention, command inversion, and serialization.
tasks.definition_of_done: Randomized sequences preserve invariants and round-trip state.

tasks.task: Publish timeline-core API documentation and compatibility fixtures.
tasks.definition_of_done: UI, server, adapters, bridge, render, and QA consume one versioned contract.

tasks.task: Implement asset registration with stable IDs, canonical/project-relative paths, file hashes, media family, rights metadata, and validation status.
tasks.definition_of_done: Assets are authoritative in assets.json and survive cache deletion.

tasks.task: Implement secure path normalization, traversal rejection, project-root policy, approved external asset references, and missing-path diagnostics.
tasks.definition_of_done: No asset operation escapes approved roots.

tasks.task: Integrate ffprobe/media inspection for container, codec, dimensions, rational FPS, duration, time base, sample rate, channels, alpha, and variable-frame-rate status.
tasks.definition_of_done: Probe results are cached by content hash and validated.

tasks.task: Implement duplicate detection by hash and source-change detection by path plus content identity.
tasks.definition_of_done: Duplicates and changed files are visible without silently substituting sources.

tasks.task: Implement variable-frame-rate detection, constant-frame-rate proxy/mezzanine generation, and explicit source-to-proxy time maps.
tasks.definition_of_done: Frame-accurate editing never trusts VFR timestamps directly.

tasks.task: Implement configurable proxy profiles, background jobs, original/proxy switching, relink, invalidation, and final-source guard.
tasks.definition_of_done: Final render preflight rejects accidental proxy use.

tasks.task: Implement thumbnail, contact-sheet, filmstrip, and waveform generation with content-addressed caching.
tasks.definition_of_done: Generated views are cancelable, restartable, and invalidated by source/profile changes.

tasks.task: Implement font registration, hash resolution, local bundle policy, missing-font diagnostics, and project font manifest.
tasks.definition_of_done: Preview, fidelity capture, and final render resolve identical font files.

tasks.task: Implement asset search/filter/sort by name, type, duration, resolution, rights, status, date, and usage count.
tasks.definition_of_done: Search indexes are rebuildable and support UI pagination/virtualization.

tasks.task: Implement replace/relink flows, usage reports, reveal-in-filesystem action, approved/rejected/favorite states, and duplicate review.
tasks.definition_of_done: Relinking updates a reversible project transaction and invalidates dependent artifacts.

tasks.task: Implement rights, source URL, creator, license, restrictions, attribution, proof, expiry, and review-date fields with delivery preflight hooks.
tasks.definition_of_done: Unreviewed rights can warn or block according to profile policy.

tasks.task: Build corrupt media, missing media, VFR, alpha, font, proxy, relink, duplicate, rights, and cache-deletion fixtures.
tasks.definition_of_done: Media milestone tests produce deterministic diagnostics and recovery behavior.

tasks.task: Implement loopback-only server bootstrap, port selection, single-instance/project-instance policy, local session token, origin checks, and startup health report.
tasks.definition_of_done: The server is not reachable from LAN by default and rejects unauthorized local origins.

tasks.task: Define versioned API schemas and a common success/error envelope with correlation ID, stage, entity, retryability, and repair hints.
tasks.definition_of_done: All HTTP/CLI/bridge operations share error semantics.

tasks.task: Implement project endpoints for create, open, close, list recent, snapshot, revision history, named versions, migration report, and repair report.
tasks.definition_of_done: Endpoints never mutate authoritative files outside the command layer.

tasks.task: Implement command endpoint with baseRevisionId, lock handling, validation, commit, conflict response, undo, and redo.
tasks.definition_of_done: A command either commits one coordinated revision or commits nothing.

tasks.task: Implement asset endpoints for import, inspect, proxy, thumbnail, waveform, relink, replace, rights, search, and usage.
tasks.definition_of_done: Long operations return jobs and stream progress.

tasks.task: Implement preview/session endpoints for load, transport, seek, frame-step, quality, preload, state, and adapter diagnostics.
tasks.definition_of_done: Commands are serialized through the authoritative scheduler contract.

tasks.task: Implement selection, context, capture, annotation, comparison, and source-edit transaction endpoints.
tasks.definition_of_done: Read operations may be automatic; mutations require command authorization.

tasks.task: Implement render, queue, cancellation, retry, outputs, QA, approval, and receipt endpoints as contract placeholders before render-core completion.
tasks.definition_of_done: UI can integrate against stable mocked schemas while backend stages mature.

tasks.task: Implement WebSocket or SSE event stream for revision, selection, transport, buffering, dropped frames, asset jobs, render progress, QA state, warnings, and worker health.
tasks.definition_of_done: Events are ordered per stream, resumable where required, and include correlation IDs.

tasks.task: Implement worker process supervisor, typed RPC, heartbeats, crash restart, resource limits, cancellation, and log capture.
tasks.definition_of_done: A worker crash cannot corrupt project state or bring down the server.

tasks.task: Use SQLite only for regenerable job/cache/search indexes and implement rebuild-on-delete behavior.
tasks.definition_of_done: Deleting SQLite state loses no creative or approval data.

tasks.task: Implement disk-space preflight, temp-root management, orphan cleanup, file watcher, external-change quarantine notification, and graceful shutdown.
tasks.definition_of_done: Shutdown/cancel/restart leaves valid revisions and cache artifacts intact.

tasks.task: Bootstrap React, TypeScript, and Vite application with route/workspace boundaries and error isolation.
tasks.definition_of_done: A component failure cannot blank the entire editor without recovery action.

tasks.task: Select and document client state separation for server snapshots, transient interaction state, transport state, command state, and cached queries.
tasks.definition_of_done: Authoritative project state is never duplicated as an uncontrolled mutable client model.

tasks.task: Implement API client, event subscription, reconnect/resync logic, correlation IDs, stale-revision detection, and typed mocks.
tasks.definition_of_done: UI recovers from server/stream restart without silently losing selection or edits.

tasks.task: Implement design-token package and shared components specified in P01.
tasks.definition_of_done: Components pass visual, keyboard, focus, disabled, busy, warning, and error state checks.

tasks.task: Implement top bar with project/revision identity, workspace switcher, authoritative timecode, preview mode, render status, capture, and render actions.
tasks.definition_of_done: Status labels derive from real state and never fake success.

tasks.task: Implement resizable/collapsible panel system, minimum sizes, saved workspace layouts, reset layout, and monitor/timeline dominance rules.
tasks.definition_of_done: Layouts remain usable at the supported minimum desktop viewport.

tasks.task: Implement global command/shortcut router with focus scopes, tool modes, text-input exclusions, conflict display, and later configurability.
tasks.definition_of_done: Keyboard actions match visible command state and are screen-reader discoverable.

tasks.task: Implement toasts, inline validation, blocking dialogs, progress surfaces, repair actions, detailed-log drawers, and no-fake-success policy.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every server error category has a visible, actionable presentation.

tasks.task: Implement empty/loading/offline/reconnecting/migrating/recovering/read-only/conflict states for each workspace.
tasks.definition_of_done: State gallery and visual tests cover all non-happy paths.

tasks.task: Add UI performance instrumentation for render cost, long tasks, virtual-list load, event lag, and interaction latency.
tasks.definition_of_done: Development diagnostics identify slow components without sending telemetry externally.

tasks.task: Implement transport state machine for stopped, loading, paused, playing, seeking, buffering, error, and disposed states.
tasks.definition_of_done: Invalid transitions are rejected and every state is observable.

tasks.task: Implement authoritative masterFrame, rational presentation timestamp, session ID, play rate, loop range, in/out range, and frame/second stepping.
tasks.definition_of_done: No attached adapter advances authoritative time independently.

tasks.task: Implement seek barriers that halt adapters/audio, request the exact frame, await readiness, present atomically, and report partial failures.
tasks.definition_of_done: Repeated seek to one frame yields the same intended composite.

tasks.task: Implement synchronized playback sessions, adapter frame reports, drift calculation, dropped-frame accounting, hard resync, and session cancellation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Long mixed-engine fixture stays within measured drift policy.

tasks.task: Implement preload windows, buffering model, buffered-range aggregation, cache freshness state, and back-pressure.
tasks.definition_of_done: UI can distinguish waiting for media, engine, render fallback, and audio.

tasks.task: Implement interactive quality policies for proxy/original, resolution scale, disabled expensive effects, fallback layers, and load-based degradation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Quality changes are explicit and never relabeled as fidelity.

tasks.task: Implement preview layer graph with deterministic z-order, common transforms, opacity, crop, blend mode, letterbox/pillarbox, guides, and annotation overlays.
tasks.definition_of_done: Remotion, HyperFrames, and shared layers composite in timeline order.

tasks.task: Implement native-layer, shared-layer, and baked-fallback lifecycle with preload, present, suspend, error, and disposal boundaries.
tasks.definition_of_done: A failing layer yields a visible error/fallback without corrupting transport.

tasks.task: Implement rendered-fidelity frame and short-range requests through the final-compositor contract.
tasks.definition_of_done: Fidelity results record frame, compositor, settings, environment, color, alpha, and dependency identity.

tasks.task: Implement preview warnings for proxy, baked fallback, unsupported effect, missing asset/font, stale cache, buffering, dropped frames, and render-required difference.
tasks.definition_of_done: Warning state reaches server events and UI with direct remedy actions.

tasks.task: Implement color/alpha normalization contract for exact comparisons.
tasks.definition_of_done: Strict environment deterministic fixtures compare by normalized pixel hash.

tasks.task: Implement audio transport hooks, barriers, latency/drift reporting, scrub/rate policy, and native-engine audio suppression against the frozen audio-transport contract.
tasks.definition_of_done: Preview scheduler exposes one authoritative session interface that the shared audio graph follows without circular ownership.

tasks.task: Build repeated seek, play/pause/scrub, loop, quality-change, layer-failure, drift, fallback, and fidelity integration tests.
tasks.definition_of_done: Unified preview milestone gate passes the mixed-engine fixture.

tasks.task: Publish preview-core and layer-adapter contracts with compatibility test harness.
tasks.definition_of_done: Engine adapters can be upgraded independently against one conformance suite.

tasks.task: Implement composition discovery, metadata extraction, dimensions/FPS/duration validation, and input-prop schema discovery.
tasks.definition_of_done: Invalid or ambiguous compositions produce structured diagnostics.

tasks.task: Implement source validation for component path, props, delayed rendering, fonts, assets, unsupported configuration, and version compatibility.
tasks.definition_of_done: Validation runs without starting a final render.

tasks.task: Implement Player host lifecycle, master-frame seek, readiness barrier, current-frame reporting, suspend, and disposal.
tasks.definition_of_done: Player never owns the project clock.

tasks.task: Implement synchronized playback optimization under the preview session contract.
tasks.definition_of_done: Native playback is disabled or resynced when drift exceeds policy.

tasks.task: Implement exact still rendering with declared frame, props, composition, browser, color, alpha, and environment identity.
tasks.definition_of_done: Repeated strict-environment stills match normalized hashes.

tasks.task: Implement range/video rendering, progress, cancellation, logs, and artifact metadata.

tasks.definition_of_done: Golden updates require reviewed evidence.

tasks.task: Implement caption readability, collision, safe-zone, line length, reading speed, and phrase-sync checks.
tasks.definition_of_done: Caption failures link to exact cue and frame range.

tasks.task: Implement VO phrase-to-visual anchor, caption-to-transcript, music/SFX cue, long-timeline drift, and cross-engine boundary sync checks.
tasks.definition_of_done: Sync reports use frame/sample deltas, not vague pass/fail.

tasks.task: Implement the authoritative QA/approval/delivery lifecycle service from the frozen transition contract.
tasks.definition_of_done: Invalid transitions are impossible through API, CLI, UI, render workers, receipt import, or direct service calls; output changes invalidate relevant QA.

tasks.task: Implement visual review checklist generation for boundary frames, phrase anchors, transition midpoints, captions, alpha, shaders, continuity, color, first/last.
tasks.definition_of_done: Required human checks are recorded as evidence, not implicit.

tasks.task: Implement accepted-exception application, expiration, scope matching, and receipt inclusion.
tasks.definition_of_done: An exception cannot suppress unrelated/new failures.

tasks.task: Build QA rule unit tests, fixture integration tests, parity tests, sync tests, state-transition tests, and delivery-bypass security tests.
tasks.definition_of_done: QA hardening gate passes all non-negotiable acceptance tests.

tasks.task: Create threat model for local server, browser UI, project files, imported media, executable compositions, bridge/CLI, render workers, caches, logs, outputs, and dependencies.
tasks.definition_of_done: Threats map to controls, tests, and residual risks.

tasks.task: Enforce loopback binding, explicit LAN opt-in only if later approved, origin/token checks, CSRF-safe mutations, and no broad browser access.
tasks.definition_of_done: Network scans from another host cannot reach the default server.

tasks.task: Implement canonical path policy, project-root/approved-external allowlists, symlink handling, traversal rejection, read-only mounts, and safe temp/output roots.
tasks.definition_of_done: Workers cannot read/write outside approved paths.

tasks.task: Implement trust classification for trusted_authored and imported_untrusted compositions with explicit promotion review.
tasks.definition_of_done: Trust state is authoritative and appears in inspector/preflight/receipt.

tasks.task: Implement network deny-by-default, per-project/domain allowlist, immutable fetched-resource hashing, and final-render reproducibility rules.
tasks.definition_of_done: Unapproved network access fails visibly and cannot enter cache.

tasks.task: Implement environment-variable allowlist with secrets excluded, sanitized process environment, locale/timezone control, and receipt identity.
tasks.definition_of_done: Workers receive only declared variables.

tasks.task: Implement CSP, navigation, popup, download, external-protocol, file URL, local-service, clipboard, and browser-permission restrictions.
tasks.definition_of_done: Violation attempts are blocked and logged.

tasks.task: Implement isolated subprocess/container/sandbox profile per supported platform with CPU, memory, wall-time, process-count, file-output, and log-size limits.
tasks.definition_of_done: Resource-exhaustion fixtures terminate safely.

tasks.task: Separate trusted/untrusted worker pools, browser profiles, temp roots, caches, and artifact provenance.
tasks.definition_of_done: Cross-policy cache reuse is impossible.

tasks.task: Implement secret/PII/path redaction for logs, bridge manifests, diagnostics bundles, and receipts while preserving useful project-relative context.
tasks.definition_of_done: Redaction tests cover tokens, home paths, unrelated files, and environment data.

tasks.task: Implement destructive-operation authorization for source deletion, project-wide replacement, cache cleanup scope, and external publishing/uploading.
tasks.definition_of_done: Publishing remains outside baseline and cannot occur implicitly.

tasks.task: Create exact dependency/license inventory for Remotion, HyperFrames, FFmpeg build/codecs, fonts, assets, browser, and bundled libraries.
tasks.definition_of_done: Inventory records source, version, license, notices, and distribution obligations.

tasks.task: Add release-blocking license review workflow for public distribution, commercialization, team/automation changes, and engine upgrades.
tasks.definition_of_done: No release assumes architectural abstraction alone guarantees compliance.

tasks.task: Build path escape, symlink, network, environment, popup/navigation, resource exhaustion, cache contamination, log leakage, CSRF/origin, and authorization tests.
tasks.definition_of_done: Imported-composition support stays blocked until all mandatory containment tests pass.

tasks.task: Implement startup health checks for browser, engine versions, adapters, FFmpeg, codecs, GPU/backend, fonts, write permissions, disk, and project integrity.
tasks.definition_of_done: Startup distinguishes blocking, degraded, and repairable states.

tasks.task: Implement project repair scanner that reports orphan revisions, invalid pointer, stale lock, missing assets/fonts, unwrapped source edits, corrupt cache, and incomplete jobs before changing anything.
tasks.definition_of_done: Repair never runs silently.

tasks.task: Implement explicit repair actions for pointer recovery, orphan adoption/rejection, stale-lock clear, asset relink, cache purge, job cleanup, and autosave restore.
tasks.definition_of_done: Each repair creates evidence and avoids deleting sources.

tasks.task: Implement resumable render stages and validated artifact reuse after server/worker restart.
tasks.definition_of_done: Restart resumes only complete, valid nodes.

tasks.task: Implement safe cancellation cleanup, temp-file retention policy, orphan cleanup, cache cleanup limits, and source-protection checks.
tasks.definition_of_done: Cleanup cannot delete user sources or approved outputs.

tasks.task: Implement local structured log rotation, correlation search, stage timing, memory/concurrency metrics, media probes, engine console logs, and cache reasons.
tasks.definition_of_done: Diagnostics remain local by default.

tasks.task: Implement user-facing diagnostics with plain summary, affected entity, exact stage/frame, suggested repair, safe retry, source inspect, and detailed log drawer.
tasks.definition_of_done: Common failures are solvable without opening raw logs.

tasks.task: Implement exportable diagnostics bundle with explicit user selection and redaction preview.
tasks.definition_of_done: Bundle excludes project media/secrets by default.

tasks.task: Implement crash reporter limited to local dump/log artifacts and opt-in future telemetry boundary.
tasks.definition_of_done: No diagnostics are transmitted automatically.

tasks.task: Run fault injection at every revision-write, cache-publish, render-stage, encode-finalize, receipt-write, and approval transition boundary.
tasks.definition_of_done: System always resolves to a valid prior or complete new state.

tasks.task: Run low-disk, corrupt-media, missing-font, browser restart, worker crash, cache deletion, concurrent command, and external-edit recovery tests.
tasks.definition_of_done: Reliability acceptance suite passes repeatedly.

tasks.task: Document backup, restore, project move, cache rebuild, repair, and recovery procedures.
tasks.definition_of_done: Procedures are validated on fixtures, not merely written.

tasks.task: Implement roll edit with adjacent-clip handle validation, linked media policy, snapping, preview, command inverse, and keyboard operation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Total sequence duration remains invariant unless explicitly allowed.

tasks.task: Implement slip edit with source-handle visualization, source monitor preview, exact rational mapping, and command inverse.
tasks.definition_of_done: Timeline range remains fixed while source range changes deterministically.

tasks.task: Implement slide edit with neighbor constraints, linked media, snapping, and command inverse.
tasks.definition_of_done: Selected clip duration/source remains fixed while surrounding boundaries update.

tasks.task: Complete the professional source monitor: source in/out, source patching and target tracks, insert, overwrite, replace edit, and full three-point editing with independent source transport.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every source/timeline range combination is a validated reversible command; source transport remains separate from the master timeline clock.

tasks.task: Implement compound/nested clip creation, open-in-timeline, rate mapping, flatten/bake option, and dependency propagation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Nested timing remains rational and reversible.

tasks.task: Implement alternate takes/version stacks with active-take switching and review comparison.
tasks.definition_of_done: Inactive takes remain referenced without affecting render dependencies.

tasks.task: Implement freeze frame and reverse playback/render with exact source-frame policy and audio defaults.
tasks.definition_of_done: Boundary sampling is documented and tested.

tasks.task: Implement constant speed changes and duration/source-range reconciliation using normalized rational speed ratios.
tasks.definition_of_done: No floating-point speed authority is persisted.

tasks.task: Implement time remapping and speed curves with monotonic/non-monotonic policy, frame sampling, handles, and audio behavior.
tasks.definition_of_done: Render and preview evaluate identical mapping rules.

tasks.task: Implement adjustment layers and range-based effects with capability planning, common/native ownership, and cache invalidation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Unsupported cross-engine effects require explicit bake/fallback.

tasks.task: Implement advanced transition/bridge editor for shader/custom transitions, ownership, handles, alpha, pre/post-roll, audio envelopes, fallback, exact range render, and boundary QA.
tasks.definition_of_done: Experimental paths cannot ship without fallback and validation.

tasks.task: Complete keyframe curve editor with multi-property selection, align/distribute, retime, copy/paste, value/speed graphs, tangent modes, and zoom/navigation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Curves round-trip and evaluate deterministically.

tasks.task: Expand audio automation UI with lane editing, bus automation, ducking controls, crossfade editor, sync anchors, and meter history.
tasks.definition_of_done: Automation edits remain reversible and sample-aligned.

tasks.task: Implement range-based effects, adjustment metadata, render/cache visualization, and inspector affected-range previews.
tasks.definition_of_done: Dependency invalidation is limited to affected ranges/artifacts.

tasks.task: Build advanced edit property/fuzz tests, UI interactions, preview/render parity, undo/reopen, nested timing, audio, bridge, and cache tests.
tasks.definition_of_done: Professional expansion gate passes without relaxing foundation guarantees.

tasks.task: Create supported hardware/project classes and benchmark fixtures for small, medium, long, hundreds-of-clips, heavy WebGL, captions, audio, and mixed-engine bridges.
tasks.definition_of_done: Performance claims identify the tested class and environment.

tasks.task: Instrument cold start, project open, seek, frame-step, play drift, timeline interaction, exact capture, proxy generation, render throughput, memory, GPU, disk, and cache hit rate.
tasks.definition_of_done: Metrics are reproducible and remain local.

tasks.task: Optimize project snapshot loading, derived-index rebuild, schema validation, and revision diff without weakening whole-project validation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Large fixtures open within frozen budget.

tasks.task: Optimize timeline virtualization, clip drawing, thumbnails/waveforms, hit testing, drag previews, search, and inspector updates.
tasks.definition_of_done: Interaction latency remains within baseline under stress fixture.

tasks.task: Optimize preview preloading, adapter lifecycle, browser reuse, proxy policy, quality scale, worker concurrency, and GPU resource ownership.
tasks.definition_of_done: Optimizations preserve deterministic transport/capture contracts.

tasks.task: Optimize cache storage, cleanup, lookup, dependency hashing, and selective invalidation.
tasks.definition_of_done: Cache speed does not trade correctness.

tasks.task: Implement honest degradation ladder: dropped-frame report, lower preview quality, disable expensive preview effects, render preview range, and never claim frame-perfect real time.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every degradation is visible and reversible.

tasks.task: Complete configurable shortcut editor, shortcut search, conflict resolution, import/export, and reset.
tasks.definition_of_done: Core operations remain keyboard accessible after customization.

tasks.task: Complete screen-reader semantics, timeline summaries, focus order/restoration, high-contrast states, reduced motion, scalable text, and non-color-only indicators.
tasks.definition_of_done: Accessibility test matrix passes for every workspace.

tasks.task: Run soak/stress tests for long playback, repeated seek, hundreds of clips, long render, cancel/retry, low disk, corrupt media, browser restart, and cache cleanup.
tasks.definition_of_done: No state corruption or unbounded resource growth occurs.

tasks.task: Freeze production budgets and regression thresholds per supported environment.
tasks.definition_of_done: CI/performance review blocks material regressions or requires documented acceptance.

tasks.task: Define supported OS/hardware/browser/GPU matrix and the exact local launch model without requiring a desktop wrapper or cloud account.
tasks.definition_of_done: Support claims match tested environments.

tasks.task: Create reproducible build/release pipeline with lockfile verification, generated-schema checks, tests, fixture renders, license inventory, notices, checksums, and signed release manifest where appropriate.
tasks.definition_of_done: Release artifact can be traced to source, dependencies, and test evidence.

tasks.task: Create local installer/bootstrap scripts or package instructions for Node/runtime, browser, FFmpeg, project permissions, and first launch.
tasks.definition_of_done: Fresh supported machine reaches a healthy local Studio without manual source edits.

tasks.task: Implement version/about/diagnostics screen showing Studio, schema, engines, adapters, browser, FFmpeg, compositor, environment fingerprint, and support status.
tasks.definition_of_done: Displayed identity matches receipts and logs.

tasks.task: Document user workflows for projects, import, editing, preview fidelity, capture/Codex context, render, QA, approval, versions, backup, restore, relink, and repair.
tasks.definition_of_done: Documentation uses current UI and tested procedures.

tasks.task: Document developer architecture, schemas, commands, adapters, capability registry, workers, isolation, render DAG, cache, QA, fixtures, and debugging.
tasks.definition_of_done: A new maintainer can trace one edit and one render end to end.

tasks.task: Implement engine/adaptor version pin policy and isolated upgrade branch/worktree workflow.
tasks.definition_of_done: One engine family upgrades at a time.

tasks.task: Automate upgrade checks: type/unit, adapter contract, fixture render, golden compare, audio sync, performance, capability registry diff, security, license, and written receipt.
tasks.definition_of_done: An upstream update cannot enter production without evidence.

tasks.task: Implement schema migration release notes, compatibility matrix, project backup, rollback policy, and unsupported-version messaging.
tasks.definition_of_done: Users can understand irreversible migration boundaries before opening.

tasks.task: Create example projects for raw media, Remotion, HyperFrames, mixed engine, captions, audio, bridges, alpha, untrusted import, and professional edits.
tasks.definition_of_done: Examples double as documentation and regression fixtures.

tasks.task: Create release candidate checklist covering clean install, open/reopen, core/pro edits, bridge context, fidelity capture, render/cache, QA, receipt reproduction, crash recovery, and license review.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every release candidate produces an archived evidence bundle.

tasks.task: Keep desktop wrapper as a post-stability ADR only; do not introduce wrapper-specific foundation dependencies.
tasks.definition_of_done: V1 remains a localhost web/server product.

tasks.task: Implement and test installation/bootstrap, `chai-studio doctor`, environment reporting, clean uninstall, and project-preservation behavior.
tasks.definition_of_done: A supported clean machine can install and launch without developer-only source edits; uninstall removes application/runtime artifacts but preserves user projects and approved outputs.

tasks.task: Implement and test project backup, restore, clone, archive, cache exclusion, delivered-artifact preservation, and cross-machine validation.
tasks.definition_of_done: A backup restored on another supported environment opens, validates, and retains immutable revisions, approvals, receipts, and delivery identity.

tasks.task: Run clean-machine qualification through create/import/edit/capture/Codex-context/render/QA/approve/deliver/reproduce and uninstall-preserve-project.
tasks.definition_of_done: The documented primary journey succeeds without local developer state or undocumented manual fixes.

tasks.task: Run release-candidate disaster drills for commit crash, render crash, stale lock, corrupt cache, missing source/font, low disk, worker/browser failure, output-permission loss, and restore from backup.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every drill produces the documented valid prior-state, resume, repair, or blocking outcome with evidence.

tasks.task: Establish post-release operations: issue triage, security response, support-bundle procedure, backup guidance, rollback, regression cadence, and engine/license re-review triggers.
tasks.definition_of_done: Ongoing maintenance cannot bypass the same change, test, security, license, and evidence gates used for Version 1.

tasks.task: Run clean-machine create/open/save/close/reopen/migrate/recover acceptance across every supported environment.
tasks.definition_of_done: Project state and timing remain identical after reopen.

tasks.task: Run mixed Remotion/HyperFrames/shared-media master-clock acceptance for repeated seek, play, pause, frame-step, loop, quality change, and long playback.
tasks.definition_of_done: Adapters remain aligned within frozen drift policy.

tasks.task: Run complete core and professional edit acceptance with undo/redo, autosave, crash recovery, named versions, and source-edit transactions.
tasks.definition_of_done: Visible and persisted state restore exactly.

tasks.task: Run media/proxy/VFR/font/relink/rights/missing/corrupt acceptance and prove proxy cannot enter final delivery silently.
tasks.definition_of_done: Blocking failures occur before final render.

tasks.task: Run program/source monitor, keyboard, selection, transcript, captions, inspector, keyframe, curve, audio, and accessibility acceptance.
tasks.definition_of_done: All major workflows are operable and states are truthful.

tasks.task: Run Codex bridge acceptance from exact selection/capture context through validated command/source edit, revised preview, compare, undo, and sanitized logs.
tasks.definition_of_done: Context contains complete source identity and stale context is rejected.

tasks.task: Run strict fidelity capture parity and approved cross-environment comparison across golden fixtures.
tasks.definition_of_done: Capture corresponds to the exact final-render frame under declared contract.

tasks.task: Run cross-engine transition/bridge acceptance for no duplicate/missing/blank boundary frames, alpha, fallback, pre/post-roll, audio envelope, and exact range.
tasks.definition_of_done: Every bridge passes boundary QA or blocks delivery.

tasks.task: Run long-timeline audio, transcript/caption, VO anchor, music/SFX cue, and cross-engine sync acceptance.
tasks.definition_of_done: Frame/sample deltas remain within frozen policy.

tasks.task: Run render DAG/cache acceptance for selective invalidation, strict fingerprint changes, corrupt cache, cancellation, retry, restart/resume, and safe cleanup.
tasks.definition_of_done: Only dependent artifacts rerender and project state remains intact.

tasks.task: Run QA state-machine and delivery-bypass acceptance, including warnings, failures, accepted exceptions, approval, and complete receipt.
tasks.definition_of_done: No output is delivered solely because encoding succeeded.

tasks.task: Reproduce an approved output from its receipt and verify output hash or approved parity contract.
tasks.definition_of_done: Receipt is sufficient for reproducible reconstruction in the declared environment.

tasks.task: Run security/isolation/authorization/redaction/license acceptance and confirm imported-untrusted support is either proven or blocked.
tasks.definition_of_done: No release contains an unverified containment promise.

tasks.task: Run performance, soak, stress, and resource-ceiling acceptance for each supported hardware/project class.
tasks.definition_of_done: Regressions are resolved or explicitly removed from support claims.

tasks.task: Complete the final product walkthrough across Edit, Inspect, Deliver, Codex bridge, mixed-engine fidelity, recovery, security status, and Professional Expansion; record and close every correction.
tasks.definition_of_done: Walkthrough findings are implemented and reverified; no approval is inferred from attendance or task counts.

tasks.task: Verify backup/restore on another supported machine and clean uninstall/reinstall while preserving projects, delivered artifacts, receipts, and recovery history.
tasks.definition_of_done: Restored/reinstalled state passes validation and reproduces the approved sample output or reports an explicit environment incompatibility.

tasks.task: Repeat the complete disaster-recovery matrix against the final release candidate, including rollback to the prior supported release.
tasks.definition_of_done: Recovery and rollback outcomes match documentation and leave no false success, mixed revision, or untrusted artifact promotion.

tasks.task: Generate the final traceability and release-evidence bundle linking every Foundation and Professional requirement to implementation, tests, fixtures, receipts, exceptions, walkthrough corrections, and approval evidence.
tasks.definition_of_done: The matrix has no unimplemented in-scope requirement, unexplained waiver, missing test, or unresolved release blocker.

tasks.task: Create the signed Version 1 release receipt, immutable version tag/manifest, artifact hashes, dependency pins, environment fingerprints, supported-environment declaration, known limitations, and explicit owner approval.
tasks.definition_of_done: Release identity is reproducible from pinned inputs and cannot be confused with planning or traceability completion.

tasks.task: Complete operational handoff and rollback drill using the final package, support procedure, security contacts, backup guidance, upgrade branch policy, and regression schedule.
tasks.definition_of_done: Version 1 is supportable after release and operations cannot bypass approved lifecycle or change-control rules.
